CREATE TABLE paths (id INT, path TEXT);
SELECT parse_dirpath(path, 'forward_slash') AS head FROM paths WHERE id = 1;
SELECT parse_dirpath(path, 'backslash') AS head FROM paths WHERE id = 2;
SELECT parse_dirpath(path, 'both_slash') AS head FROM paths WHERE id IN (3, 4);
SELECT id, parse_dirpath(path, 'both_slash') AS head FROM paths WHERE path LIKE 'C:%' OR path LIKE '/%';
WITH path_data AS (SELECT path, parse_dirpath(path, 'forward_slash') AS head FROM paths) SELECT * FROM path_data WHERE head IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE join_example AS SELECT a.id, a.path, b.head FROM paths AS a JOIN (SELECT id, parse_dirpath(path, 'forward_slash') AS head FROM paths) AS b ON a.id = b.id WHERE a.id < 3;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM paths WHERE parse_dirpath(path, 'backslash') IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, path, parse_dirpath(path, 'both_slash') AS head FROM paths ORDER BY head DESC;
CREATE TABLE mixed_paths (id INT, path TEXT);
SELECT a.id, a.path, parse_dirpath(b.path, 'forward_slash') AS head FROM mixed_paths AS a LEFT JOIN paths AS b ON a.id = b.id;
